summary: EntryPointNotFoundException for 'sk_region_op2' when FreeSpire.XLS 14.2.0 (built against SkiaSharp 2.88.x) is used alongside SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux 3.116.x, where the native symbol was renamed to 'sk_region_op' in the 3.x series.

bugSignals.errorMessage: System.EntryPointNotFoundException: Unable to find an entry point named 'sk_region_op2' in shared library 'libSkiaSharp'
bugSignals.stackTrace:
at SkiaSharp.SkiaApi.sk_region_op2(IntPtr r, IntPtr src, SKRegionOperation op)
at SkiaSharp.SKRegion.Op(SKRegion region, SKRegionOperation op)
[... Spire.Xls internal frames ...]

stepsToReproduce: Add FreeSpire.XLS 14.2.0 and SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux 3.116.1 to a .NET project

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The 3.x native library exports sk_region_op (not sk_region_op2); FreeSpire.XLS was compiled against the 2.88.x SkiaSharp managed assembly, which called sk_region_op2.

regression.reason: The 3.x SkiaSharp C API renamed sk_region_op2 to sk_region_op. FreeSpire.XLS bundles its own SkiaSharp 2.88.x managed DLL that P/Invokes the old symbol name, so upgrading native assets to 3.x breaks it.

analysis.summary: FreeSpire.XLS 14.2.0 embeds or depends on SkiaSharp 2.88.x managed bindings that P/Invoke 'sk_region_op2'. SkiaSharp 3.x renamed this C API entry point to 'sk_region_op'. The user is providing the 3.x native library which does not export 'sk_region_op2', causing an EntryPointNotFoundException at startup. This is a version mismatch between a third-party library's SkiaSharp 2.88.x dependency and the user's SkiaSharp 3.x native assets.
analysis.rationale: The error is a clear EntryPointNotFoundException for a known-renamed symbol (sk_region_op2 → sk_region_op in 3.x). Code investigation confirms SkiaSharp 3.x bindings only export sk_region_op. The crash originates from FreeSpire.XLS's internal usage of the old SkiaSharp 2.88.x P/Invoke binding. A maintainer comment confirmed FreeSpire.XLS uses an older Skia version. The fix must come from FreeSpire.XLS updating to SkiaSharp 3.x.

codeInvestigation.finding: SkiaSharp 3.x generated bindings define sk_region_op (not sk_region_op2). There is no sk_region_op2 symbol exported in the 3.x native library. The rename happened between 2.88.x and 3.x as part of the C API cleanup.
SKRegion.Op(SKRegion, SKRegionOperation) calls SkiaApi.sk_region_op — confirming the current 3.x managed binding uses sk_region_op, not sk_region_op2.

keySignals.text: Unable to find an entry point named 'sk_region_op2' in shared library 'libSkiaSharp'
keySignals.interpretation: The 3.x native library does not export sk_region_op2; FreeSpire.XLS calls the 2.88.x symbol name.

keySignals.text: It works fine in the previous version: SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies version 2.88.9
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms FreeSpire.XLS was built against SkiaSharp 2.88.x which still exported sk_region_op2.

keySignals.text: Spire.Xls looks like it uses a much older version of Skia - I'd probably not expect the sample to work.
keySignals.interpretation: Third-party library compatibility gap — FreeSpire.XLS has not updated to SkiaSharp 3.x.

workarounds: Pin SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux to version 2.88.9 to match what FreeSpire.XLS expects, until FreeSpire.XLS publishes a version that supports SkiaSharp 3.x.
Contact E-iceblue (FreeSpire vendor) and request an update to FreeSpire.XLS that targets SkiaSharp 3.x.
nextQuestions: Has FreeSpire.XLS published a newer version (>14.2.0) that targets SkiaSharp 3.x?
Is it feasible to add a SkiaSharp 2.88.x compatibility shim that re-exports sk_region_op2 as an alias?

resolution.hypothesis: FreeSpire.XLS 14.2.0 was compiled against SkiaSharp 2.88.x and P/Invokes sk_region_op2 which no longer exists in the SkiaSharp 3.x native library.

proposals.title: Pin native assets to 2.88.9
proposals.description: Downgrade SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux to 2.88.9 to match FreeSpire.XLS's SkiaSharp 2.88.x dependency. This is a stopgap until FreeSpire.XLS upgrades.

proposals.title: Update FreeSpire.XLS
proposals.description: Check if a newer version of FreeSpire.XLS supports SkiaSharp 3.x, or request the vendor update the dependency.

resolution.recommendedReason: Immediate workaround that resolves the issue; the root fix must come from FreeSpire.XLS vendor.

actionability.reason: The EntryPointNotFoundException is caused by FreeSpire.XLS (a third-party library) using the old SkiaSharp 2.88.x P/Invoke symbol sk_region_op2 with the 3.x native library. SkiaSharp correctly renamed the symbol in 3.x. The fix must come from FreeSpire.XLS updating its SkiaSharp dependency.

actions.description: Apply bug, native, linux, and compatibility labels
Explain the version mismatch and provide the workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed report and repro project!

This error occurs because **FreeSpire.XLS 14.2.0 was built against SkiaSharp 2.88.x**, which exported a native function called `sk_region_op2`. In SkiaSharp 3.x, this function was renamed to `sk_region_op`. When the 3.x native library is loaded, `sk_region_op2` no longer exists, causing the `EntryPointNotFoundException`.

**Workaround:** Pin your `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux` package to version **2.88.9** to match what FreeSpire.XLS expects:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.NoDependencies" Version="2.88.9" />
```

The permanent fix needs to come from **E-iceblue** (the FreeSpire.XLS vendor) updating their library to target SkiaSharp 3.x. We recommend contacting them or checking their changelog for a newer version.

Because the root cause is in a third-party library, we're closing this as external. If SkiaSharp needs to re-export `sk_region_op2` as a compatibility alias, please reopen with that specific request.

actions.description: Close as external — fix must come from FreeSpire.XLS vendor
